Description: Eliminate fabricated specificity at the brief generation layer. Exit criteria: zero unsourced proper nouns in a 50-brief sample; provenance attribution reflects real sources. Source: docs/brief-excellence-plan-2026-07-05.md Phase 1.

Summary: Evidence-count grounding gate for intel-heavy prep sections
Description: Count injected evidence (signals, documents, notes, scan results) per prep section. Below threshold, switch the prompt to pattern-language mode and label the section 'pattern analysis, not verified intel' with an add-evidence CTA. Acceptance Criteria: low-evidence sections never name people or dated events; label renders in prep UI; CTA routes to company documents/notes.

Summary: Ground the onboarding intel brief with live signals
Description: Inject company_signals for the named company into the onboarding intel prompt when available; keep the existing pattern-language rule as the floor. Acceptance Criteria: a grounded signal reference appears when signals exist; output contains no fabricated names or dated events when they do not.

Summary: Proper-noun evidence validator for generated briefs
Description: Server-side post-generation check: named people and dated events must match an injected evidence source or be rewritten to pattern language before persistence. Acceptance Criteria: zero unsourced proper nouns across a 50-brief regression sample; validator failures logged to llm_traces.
Dependencies: Evidence-count grounding gate for intel-heavy prep sections

Summary: Attribution v2 with model-emitted source tagging
Description: Require generated claims tagged with source block IDs from the injected context; validate tags against context; classify unmatched claims as inferred; replace keyword-heuristic origin classification in prep provenance. Acceptance Criteria: origin classes derive from validated tags; confidence score reflects real attribution; brief quality ratings hold within 10% of baseline.
Dependencies: Proper-noun evidence validator for generated briefs

Description: Gates and provenance travel with every exported brief artifact. Exit criteria: no export path bypasses gates; exports carry a sources appendix; version history visible. Source: docs/brief-excellence-plan-2026-07-05.md Phase 2.

Block brief download/copy when sensitivePolicyHooks is non-empty until each flagged claim is reviewed; strip inferred sensitive claims entirely. Acceptance Criteria: no export path (DOCX, copy, plaintext) bypasses the gate; stripped claims logged.
Dependencies: Attribution v2 with model-emitted source tagging

Description: Require explicit acknowledgment at the export decision point when confidence band is low, with the remediation list shown. Acceptance Criteria: acknowledgment recorded per export; remediation list matches the confidence scorer output.
Dependencies: Attribution v2 with model-emitted source tagging

Summary: Provenance appendix in DOCX and plaintext exports
Description: Append a sources-and-confidence page to every export: origin-class counts, evidence sources used, generation date, confidence band. Acceptance Criteria: every export carries the appendix; appendix matches on-screen provenance state at export time.
Dependencies: Attribution v2 with model-emitted source tagging

Description: Surface prior generations per company/section from the briefs table with comparison and restore. Acceptance Criteria: regeneration never destroys visible history; restore reproduces the prior artifact exactly.

One-page default brief, dark editorial shell on the paid surface, candidate fingerprint enforcement, interviewer-specific prep. Exit criteria: Tonight view default; luxury static gate passes on prep surface; fingerprint validator at 100%. Source: docs/brief-excellence-plan-2026-07-05.md Phase 3.

Summary: Tonight view as one-page default prep brief
Description: Build a one-page default view: Bottom Line, top three pushbacks, five likely questions, How to Close; printable; full dossier one click deeper. Acceptance Criteria: Tonight view is the default landing state; renders on one screen and one printed page; dossier reachable in one click.

Summary: Dark editorial shell for the paid prep surface
Description: Port the demo renderer (slate-950, orange section headers, mono micro-labels) to prep-client; keep provenance badges legible on dark. Acceptance Criteria: prep surface passes the luxury static gate; visual parity with or above the public demo brief.

Enforce server-side that no brief section renders without at least one candidate-specific anchor (career history, STAR story, or positioning line). Acceptance Criteria: 100% of new briefs pass; violations regenerate the offending section automatically.
Dependencies: Proper-noun evidence validator for generated briefs

Description: When a contact is attached to the company, generate a stakeholder read for that person using the Stakeholder Signal Map frame. Acceptance Criteria: contact-attached briefs include the interviewer read; pattern-language floor applies when contact data is thin; nothing fabricated about the named person.
Dependencies: Evidence-count grounding gate for intel-heavy prep sections

Description: The daily brief reads like intelligence and never degrades silently. Exit criteria: briefing week-1 ritual adoption at or above 35% of activated users; zero silent fallbacks. Source: docs/brief-excellence-plan-2026-07-05.md Phase 4.

Summary: Signal-density model tiering for the daily briefing
Description: Use Sonnet on days with signals or new matches and Haiku on quiet days; raise the content cap on signal days. Acceptance Criteria: signal-day briefings measurably denser; cost delta tracked; quiet-day behavior unchanged.

Summary: Honest fallback label and signal freshness stamps
Description: Add a visible label to the non-AI credit-exhaustion fallback and a per-signal freshness stamp (detected N hours/days ago). Acceptance Criteria: zero silent fallbacks; every signal shows freshness.

Summary: Thesis relevance line in the daily briefing
Description: Add one line per briefing connecting today's items to the user's role thesis, grounded in stored role/persona context. Acceptance Criteria: line present whenever thesis context exists; never generic filler.

Brief efficacy is measured and generation cost is governed. Exit criteria: outcome captured on at least 40% of used briefs; per-activated-user generation cost known and under target. Source: docs/brief-excellence-plan-2026-07-05.md Phase 5.

Add one-tap outcome capture (advanced / rejected / offer) attached to brief lifecycle; prompt appears on next visit after used_at is set. Acceptance Criteria: outcome captured on at least 40% of used briefs; outcome stored on the brief row.

Correlate interview outcomes with brief usage in llm_traces and the evals pipeline; produce an internal monthly efficacy report; feed failure categories into the golden set. Acceptance Criteria: first monthly report produced; golden set gains outcome-labeled records.

Implement context-hash section caching so unchanged context never regenerates, regeneration metering on trial accounts, and a monthly per-user generation cost report against plan price. Acceptance Criteria: cache hit path verified; trial metering active; cost report exists.
